race.name: Great Ski Race
race.tagline: 26 kilometers from Tahoe City to Truckee across the Sierra Nevada ridge — the West Coast's largest cross-country ski race and a beloved fundraiser since 1977.

vitals.registration: Opens in fall; typically sells out
vitals.aid_stations: Aid stations along the course with drinks and snacks

climate.description: March in the Sierra brings highly variable conditions. The range can receive massive snowfall or enjoy spring-like warmth. Temperatures typically range from -5°C to 8°C. The course crosses ridgeline terrain exposed to Pacific storms. Sierra cement — heavy, wet snow — is common in spring.
challenges: Sierra Nevada weather is notoriously unpredictable
Heavy wet snow ('Sierra cement') affects glide and wax
Ridgeline exposure to Pacific storm winds
Race has been cancelled or shortened due to extreme weather

course.primary: Point-to-point from Tahoe City to Truckee, crossing the North Lake Tahoe ridge. The course traverses backcountry terrain with significant climbing in the first half and a long descent into Truckee. Stunning Sierra Nevada views when conditions permit.
course.surface: Groomed backcountry trails connecting Tahoe City to Truckee
course.grooming: Good grooming for race day, though backcountry sections can be variable
features: Point-to-point crossing of the Sierra Nevada ridge
West Coast's largest cross-country ski race
Fundraiser for Nordic search and rescue (Tahoe Nordic SAR)
Stunning Lake Tahoe and Sierra Nevada views
Both classic and freestyle racers on course together

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Spectacular Sierra Nevada scenery and strong community spirit define this event. The Lake Tahoe location provides excellent accessibility. Snow reliability is the weak link — cancellations have occurred. Shorter distance limits the endurance challenge.

history.summary: The Great Ski Race has been a North Lake Tahoe tradition since 1977, making it one of the oldest XC ski races in the western United States. Originally conceived as a fundraiser for the Tahoe Nordic Search and Rescue team, it has grown into the West Coast's largest cross-country ski event. The race traverses the ridge between Tahoe City and Truckee, retracing a historic route used by 19th-century mail carriers.
notable_facts: West Coast's largest cross-country ski race since 1977
Fundraiser for Tahoe Nordic Search and Rescue
Route follows historic mail carrier trails between Tahoe City and Truckee
Has been cancelled or modified in low-snow years
North Lake Tahoe is a major winter sports destination

videos.title: Lake Tahoe: The Great Ski Race 2017
videos.description:
Brian and Bill venture out to Lake Tahoe in early March 2017 for an epic 4-day cross-country ski trip -- and the 40th annual Great Ski Race from Tahoe City to Truckee.  Three feet of fresh snow over the weekend helped solidify this season in the history books, with over 600 inches of snow and counting.

videos.title: Cross Country skiing in bitter cold (-12° C) at Tahoe Donner Cross Country, Truckee, CA
videos.description: We enjoy a cold, crisp December day of XC skiing at the beautiful Tahoe Donner XC resort in Truckee, CA near Lake Tahoe. We pass the newly restored cowboy cabins and shake our arms to try to get some warmth in our hands! #shorts

videos.title: Mountain Biking Tahoe Cross Country Ski Area - Great Ski Race Trail
videos.description: Surprisingly good riding. No big climbs, and not that technical. This section is from the Fiberboard Road at the top of The Great Ski Race Trail down. There are a bunch of humps where one could get air, but as you can tell I like to keep my tires on the ground.

videos.title: Tahoe Donner Cross Country Lunch Ski with Judy #shorts
videos.description: We ski Tahoe Donner Cross Country in Truckee, CA, a fine XC resort. Amazing trails, excellent grooming, and competent and friendly staff. Not to mention the new Cross Country Center. Have a ski then enjoy a nice lunch and maybe an adult beverage in the bar or out on the patio with the gas fireplace! #shorts

videos.title: Fastenal Parallel 45 Cup Rollerski Loppet
videos.description:
The Fastenal Parallel 45 Cup Rollerski Loppet brought together some of the best cross-country skiers of all ages in the United States for a rollerski race in downtown Minneapolis, Minnesota. The race was the kick-off to the 2019 CXC Rollerski Cup season and a prelude to the 2020 Coop FIS Cross Country World Cup Sprint Finals and Fastenal Parallel 45 Winter Festival being held in Minneapolis, Minnesota, March 2020. 

The Elite Women's field included star athletes like Olympic gold medalist Jessie Diggins, 2010 Olympian and World Championship bronze medalist Caitlin Gregg, and 2006 Olympian Carolyn Bramante. The Elite Men's field included 4-time Olympian Andy Newell, 2019 US National Champion and World Championships skier Kyle Bratrud, World Cup skier Matt Liebsch, and US Ski Team member Jakob Ellingson.

Video by Steve Barone.

videos.title: 2013 Gatineau Loppet 55k Classic Race Review
videos.description:
ActiveSteve, not even recovered from tackling the Canadian Ski Marathon the week before, heads back out with his skis on to cover another classic Canadian ski event, the Gatineau Loppet, heading into it's 35th year. To commemorate the 35th year, the Classic event featured a 55km and 38km linear (point-to-point) route instead of the normal looped course. Under brilliant skies and great snow conditions, he lined up with participants from around the world. Check out the video to see how is all unrolled.

Music: Dan-O Songs

videos.title: The American Birkebeiner cross-country ski race
videos.description:
Since it was first held 50 years ago, the American Birkebeiner, a trek of more than 30 miles through the Northwoods of Wisconsin, modeled after a fabled bit of Norwegian history, has become the largest cross-country ski race in the U.S. Correspondent Conor Knighton reports on how enthusiasm for the festival of events surrounding the race (dubbed "Birke fever") has snowballed.
#Birkebeiner #crosscountryski 

"CBS Sunday Morning" features stories on the arts, music, nature, entertainment, sports, history, science and Americana, and highlights unique human accomplishments and achievements. Check local listings for CBS Sunday Morning broadcast times.
